Article Title: Attacks on merchant shipping completely unacceptable: EAM S Jaishankar
Article Text: NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar Wednesday underlined India's strong commitment to safe and unimpeded transit passage of maritime shipping, saying attacks on merchant shipping were completely unacceptable.The minister gave vent to India's concerns related to merchant shipping disruptions in Strait of Hormuz, while participating in a meeting convened by Japan to discuss supply chain disruptions in energy markets."Global growth demands that energy markets are not constricted. As a major energy consumer, India will work with like-minded partners to develop supply chain resilience," said Jaishankar in a post on X.He was participating in the Asia Zero Emission Community Plus Online Summit on Energy Resilience that was convened by Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi.
Article Summary: S jaishankar underlined india's strong commitment to safe and unimpeded transit passage of maritime shipping. He was participating in a meeting convened by japan to discuss supply chain disruptions in energy markets. "global growth demands that energy markets are not constricted," he said.

Article Title: Average daily LPG bookings back to pre-conflict levels: Government
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The number of domestic LPG refill bookings has fallen to nearly 43-44 lakh in April from a peak of about 89 lakh in mid-March, easing pressure on cooking gas supplies, reports Atul Mathur.According to govt data, refill bookings have remained below 50 lakh cylinders since April 3, falling to 35 lakh on April 12. During this period, oil marketing companies supplied close to 50 lakh cylinders a day, except Sunday when 30.7 lakh were delivered. Between April 1 and 14, 658 lakh cylinders were booked and 652 lakh delivered.Aerage daily demand for domestic LPG was 55 lakh cylinders in the pre-crisis period.During the peak of the West Asia conflict in March, when energy flows were disrupted due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, fears of shortage triggered a surge in bookings. From an average of 57 lakh, bookings rose to 88.8 lakh on March 13, reflecting panic behaviour. The govt raised the minimum gap for refills from 15 to 21 days and then 25 days to discourage orders."Bookings vary, but on average they are 50-55 lakh a day. Domestic LPG supply is normal, and no dry-out has been reported," said Sujata Sharma, joint secretary, petroleum ministry.
Article Summary: The number of domestic lpg refill bookings has fallen to nearly 43-44 lakh in april from a peak of about 89 lakh in mid-march. The govt raised the minimum gap for refills from 15 to 21 days and then 25 days to discourage orders.

Article Title: Nicotine Gums: Experts Warn Proposed Nicotine Product Restrictions Could Hinder Smoking Cessation Efforts in India
Article Text:
India's top drug advisory body recommends restricting over-the-counter access to most nicotine replacement therapies, sparking concerns that it will hinder smokers' efforts to quit. While tobacco products remain widely available, this move could create more hurdles for those seeking quitting aids, potentially impacting public health initiatives.

NEW DELHI: Access to quitting aids like nicotine gums and lozenges may soon tighten, raising concerns among experts that it could make it harder for smokers to quit.The trigger is a recommendation by India’s top drug advisory body to restrict over-the-counter access to most nicotine replacement products. The proposal allows exemption only for unflavoured 2 mg nicotine gum, excludes nicotine lozenges (2 mg), and keeps all other nicotine replacement products outside the exemption.Currently, some of these products are available without a licence, making them easier to access. The proposed changes could mean fewer options and more hurdles for those trying to quit, even as tobacco products remain widely available.Experts say the concern stems from how addiction works. Nicotine—not the act of smoking—is the addictive substance, and withdrawal symptoms like low mood and poor concentration often push users back to cigarettes.Nicotine replacement therapies—gums, lozenges and patches—help manage these symptoms by delivering nicotine without the thousands of harmful chemicals in tobacco smoke that cause cancer, COPD and heart disease.But they are not a guaranteed solution. “Many users continue smoking along with nicotine patches or gums, which defeats the purpose and may even increase overall nicotine intake,” said Prof (Dr) GC Khilnani, chairman PSRI institute of Pulmonary and former head of pulmonary, AIIMS, Delhi.While nicotine is less harmful than tobacco smoke, it is not risk-free and can have cardiovascular effects, making medical supervision advisable, he said.India, with over 1.35 million tobacco-related deaths annually, depends heavily on accessible quitting support. Experts warn that even small barriers—like prescription requirements or limited availability—can discourage quit attempts.There are also concerns about balance. While quitting aids face tighter scrutiny, tobacco products continue to be widely sold.The panel has proposed safeguards, including a ban on sale to minors, monitoring of online sales, post-marketing surveillance, and possible restrictions on tobacco industry involvement in marketing these products due to conflict of interest concerns.However, some experts argue the risk of misuse may be overstated. Dr Priyanka Chaudhary Bindroo, Consultant Pulmonologist at Maharaja Agrasen Hospital, said, “I have not encountered a single documented case of nicotine gum or lozenge misuse by a minor. These products have been available OTC in the US and UK for decades without a public health crisis. Restricting a WHO-approved cessation aid without contemporary evidence is a step backward. ”The proposal is part of an ongoing policy debate on how to regulate nicotine products—whether to prioritise tighter control or easier access to support quitting. The final decision will be taken by the government, but for now, the key question remains: should quitting aids be made easier to access, or harder?
Article Summary: India's top drug advisory body recommends restricting over-the-counter access to most nicotine replacement therapies. The proposal allows exemption only for unflavoured 2 mg nicotine gum, excludes nicotine lozenges (2 mg) currently, some of these products are available without a licence, making them easier to access.

Article Title: BBC to axe 2,000 jobs in largest restructuring in 15 years
Article Text:
In a historic shift, the BBC is poised to reduce its workforce by nearly 2,000 positions, marking its largest cut in a decade and a half. This drastic step, which affects roughly 10% of its personnel, is in response to a £600 million budgetary initiative and dwindling licence fee revenues.







The BBC is preparing to cut up to 2,000 roles, marking its largest workforce reduction in 15 years as financial strain tightens across the organisation. The move is set to affect about 10 percent of its 21,500 employees.The restructuring comes just weeks before a leadership transition next month, when Matt Brittin, a former Google executive, formally takes charge.Staff were briefed during an all-hands meeting led by interim director general Rhodri Talfan Davies.The cuts follow a £600 million savings plan outlined earlier this year under outgoing chief Tim Davie. He had warned that the BBC needed to trim around 10 percent from its £6 billion annual cost base over three years. Davie stepped down earlier this month after facing criticism linked to editorial decisions and political scrutiny.Pressure on the BBC has been building from several directions. Licence fee income, once a stable backbone, is under strain. Although the annual fee rose to £180 in April, the number of paying households has dropped by 300,000 over the past year. Many viewers are turning to streaming platforms such as Netflix and Disney, while others avoid the fee altogether.Commercial income and grants continue to support the broadcaster, but not enough to offset the broader shift in viewing habits. Regulators have also raised concerns about the long-term future of traditional public service television, warning that it risks losing ground in an era dominated by on-demand content.Inside the organisation, there is unease about the scale of the planned cuts. Union representatives said the reduction could weaken the BBC’s ability to deliver its public service role. Staff have already faced previous rounds of job losses, and morale is under pressure as uncertainty grows.
Article Summary: The bbc is set to reduce its workforce by nearly 2,000 positions. The move will affect about 10% of its 21,500 employees. It is in response to a £600 million budgetary initiative and dwindling licence fee revenues. Staff were briefed during an all-hands meeting led by rhodri talfan davies.

Article Title: EO Charging collapse: Financial strain, global expansion blamed for shutdown
Article Text:
EO Charging collapse: Financial strain, global expansion blamed for shutdown

Collapse and Causes

How Management and Staff Are Affected

Market Standing and Broader Influence







On April 8, EO Charging stopped operating. Heavy financial strain followed its push into global markets too quickly, leaders said alongside PwC officials. Most staff lost their jobs, 69 from a total of 93. A handful stayed behind, handling closure tasks and helping current users through the exit. What began with growth plans now ends in shutdown.The company expanded operations across the United States, Australia, New Zealand and Italy in recent years. Tough markets took a toll; expenses piled up because of moves abroad.A spokesperson from PwC, Edward Williams, announced the start of administration proceedings alongside his colleague. Right away, their team shifts toward guiding clients through moves to new providers. Keeping whatever worth exists in current holdings stands as a core aim. Job losses follow, which he admitted carries weight; many people face uncertain next steps because of it.Williams took on a role beside Ross Connock and Victoria Hatton when administrators stepped in at Juuce Limited, known as EO Charging. Through the shift, staff still on board help clients move forward while handling steps to close operations.One moment it was everywhere; now it's gone. More than eighty-five thousand chargers rolled out of their factories. Over thirteen thousand public stations popped up in nearly thirty-five nations. Home efforts got a boost through team-ups, like one with a firm from Oxford. Together they wired parts of Oxfordshire for everyday charging needs.Mid-sized players face tough conditions as rivals multiply and costs climb across the EV charging landscape. Those with deeper pockets could take over independents unable to keep pace overseas.
Article Summary: Eo charging shut down on april 8. Financial strain, global expansion blamed for shutdown. Most staff lost their jobs, 69 from a total of 93. A handful stayed behind, handling closure tasks and helping current users through the exit. The company expanded operations across the united states, australia, new zealand and italy in recent years.

who is the Indian-origin contestant gaining so much attention this season?

Who is Karishma Vijay? The Indian-origin entrepreneur making headlines

The Apprentice

From contestant to finalist: a strong run in The apprentice 2026

Why Karishma Vijay is trending now

The Apprentice

Beyond the boardroom: social media, branding and influence

The Apprentice

Article Title: British households to get free electricity for weekend laundry under new scheme
Article Text:
What if weekends became a time for home energy freedom? UK's National Energy System Operator is drafting an exciting proposal that could give smart meter users free electricity on weekends! This forward-thinking initiative encourages us to embrace off-peak periods, optimizing our energy consumption while blending sustainability into our daily lives. Say hello to potential savings and a greener grid!

How the free electricity scheme works

Why surplus electricity is rising

Impact on households and daily routines

A move towards a more flexible energy system







Britons could soon receive free electricity for using household appliances at weekends, under a new scheme unveiled by the National Energy System Operator aimed at tackling rising surplus power across the grid.Announced on 14 April 2026, the initiative will reward households with smart meters for shifting their electricity use to off-peak periods, particularly when demand is low and supply is high. Activities such as running washing machines, dishwashers or charging electric vehicles during these windows could effectively cost nothing.The scheme is designed to rebalance the UK’s electricity network, where supply increasingly outpaces demand at certain times. Weekends, bank holidays and warmer days often see reduced consumption while renewable generation, especially solar, continues to produce high output.Households connected through energy suppliers or third-party platforms will be incentivised to use electricity during these surplus periods, easing pressure on the grid while reducing waste.According to the National Energy System Operator, the scheme will reward “flexible electricity use”, helping align consumption with real-time energy availability.The shift towards renewable energy is driving the increase in surplus supply, with solar generation peaking during longer daylight hours and demand failing to keep pace.At the same time, the growth of local and small-scale energy producers has reduced reliance on large power stations, creating a more distributed but less predictable energy system.In 2025, the UK spent nearly £1.5 billion managing excess wind power, including paying generators to switch off when the grid could not absorb the supply.The scheme signals a shift in how households interact with energy, not by cutting usage, but by changing when it happens. Running appliances at weekends or during off-peak hours could lead to direct savings, particularly as energy costs remain under pressure.Energy firms such as Octopus Energy have already reported rising demand for solar panels and heat pumps, reflecting a broader change in how consumers approach energy use.Officials say the initiative forms part of a wider strategy to improve grid efficiency, reducing the need for costly interventions and strengthening overall energy resilience.Dr Deborah Petterson, director of resilience and emergency management at the National Energy System Operator, said the scheme will help improve both efficiency and system stability, particularly during periods of fluctuating demand.With transmission demand expected to hit record lows this summer, the role of flexible consumption is becoming increasingly central.The scheme will be rolled out via energy suppliers and digital platforms, with participation dependent on households having smart meters installed.
Article Summary: The scheme is designed to rebalance the uk’s electricity network, where supply increasingly outpaces demand at certain times. Weekends, bank holidays and warmer days often see reduced consumption while renewable generation, especially solar, continues to produce high output.

Article Title: Snap to lay off 1,000 employees, CEO Evan Spiegel's memo puts it on AI; says: 'Rapid advancements in AI enable our teams to...'
Article Text:
Snapchat's parent company is laying off 16% of its global workforce, impacting around 1,000 employees, as it pivots towards profitable growth and integrates AI to enhance efficiency. CEO Evan Spiegel announced the move, aiming to slash costs by over $500 million by 2026 to achieve net-income profitability.

Snap's new operating model aims to spread critical work across human teams and AI agents, with 65% of new code already AI-generated

Evan Spiegel's full memo to Snap employees

Dear Team,

Today we are announcing changes that will impact approximately 1,000 team members at Snap, including 16% of our full time employees, in addition to closing more than 300 open roles. This is an incredibly difficult decision, and I am deeply sorry to the colleagues who will be leaving us. You have made important contributions to Snap, and we are committed to supporting you through this transition.

Last fall, I described Snap as facing a crucible moment, requiring a new way of working that is faster and more efficient, while pivoting towards profitable growth. Over the past several months, we have carefully reviewed the work required to best serve our community and partners, and made tough choices to prioritize the investments we believe are most likely to create long-term value. As a result of these changes, we expect to reduce our annualized cost base by more than $500 million by the second half of 2026, helping to establish a clearer path to net-income profitability.

While these changes are necessary to realize Snap's long-term potential, we believe that rapid advancements in artificial intelligence enable our teams to reduce repetitive work, increase velocity, and better support our community, partners, and advertisers. We have already witnessed small squads leveraging AI tools to drive meaningful progress across several important initiatives, including Snapchat+, enhanced ad platform performance, and efficiency improvements in our Snap Lite infrastructure.

If you are part of our North America team, please work from home today. In the US, impacted team members will receive an email notification within the next hour, including information about next steps. For non-US locations, you will receive additional details about next steps from leadership and HR.

To our departing colleagues: thank you. Your hard work has helped shape Snap, and we are deeply grateful for your contributions.

For U.S.-based team members who are leaving, we will provide four months of severance, healthcare coverage, and equity vesting, along with career transition support. Outside the U.S., we will follow local processes and seek to provide comparable support aligned with local norms.

To everyone continuing on this journey: change of this magnitude and at this speed is never easy and it will not be seamless. Thank you for your resilience, compassion, and commitment to one another, and to the community and partners we serve. Our responsibility is to move forward with clarity, empathy, and determination as we build a faster, stronger, and more durable Snap for the long term.

Evan





Snap is cutting 16% of its global workforce—around 1,000 full-time employees—as the Snapchat parent company pivots toward profitable growth and leans into AI to do more with less. CEO Evan Spiegel broke the news in an internal memo on Wednesday, also closing over 300 open roles in the process. The layoffs are expected to slash Snap's annualized cost base by over $500 million by the second half of 2026, clearing what Spiegel described as "a clearer path to net-income profitability." US-based employees leaving the company will receive four months of severance, healthcare coverage, equity vesting, and career transition support.The cuts come weeks after activist investor Irenic Capital Management—which holds roughly a 2.5% economic interest in Snap—pushed the company to optimize its portfolio and trim costs. Irenic had also urged Snap to spin off or shut down Specs, its AR smart glasses subsidiary, which has burned through over $3.5 billion in investment and costs roughly $500 million annually.Snap had about 5,261 full-time employees as of December 2025.It joins Meta, Amazon, Oracle, and Block in a wave of tech companies announcing significant job cuts this year—with several CEOs explicitly pointing to AI as reshaping workforce needs.Despite the turbulence, Snap's Q1 numbers look steady: revenue is estimated at around $1.53 billion, up 12% year-over-year, with adjusted EBITDA of $233 million—well above Wall Street's $186.8 million estimate. Snap's stock rose more than 10% in premarket trading Wednesday, even as it remains down roughly 31% for the year.Snapchat reached 946 million monthly active users in Q4 2025.
Article Summary: Snapchat's parent company is laying off 16% of its global workforce. Ceo evan spiegel announced the move, aiming to slash costs by over $500 million by 2026 to achieve net-income profitability. Snap's new operating model aims to spread critical work across human teams and ai agents.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman: “I think everyone gets a little bit caught up in the week-to-week, day-to-day, or the definitions of these abstract ideas. Just focus on…”
Article Text:
Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman

Quote of the day by Mustafa Suleyman

“I think everyone gets a little bit caught up in the week-to-week, day-to-day, or the definitions of these abstract ideas. Just focus on the capabilities. You know, we should really be thinking about these things as Artificial Capable Intelligence.

What can it do in practice, and what is the value of that “doing”? I prefer that as a framing versus AGI, because it’s … more measurable, and we can actually look at it very, very explicitly in terms of its economic impact and its impact on work.

”

The quote that shifts the conversation

What Mustafa Suleyman’s quote mean in simple terms

Why AI definitions often create confusion

A practical way to understand AI

How AI is already part of everyday life

The impact on jobs and workplaces

Measuring AI through real results

The importance of the “value of doing”

AI across different industries

A shift in how AI is being discussed

Understanding AI in a simpler way

Final insight

Poll Which application of AI do you find most beneficial? Customer service chatbots Personalized shopping recommendations Data analysis tools





Artificial intelligence is no longer something that only scientists and tech experts talk about in labs or at conferences. It has slowly become a part of daily life, changing how people work, talk to each other, shop, and even learn. AI is already having an impact on many fields, from automated chat systems to tools that help with writing and analysing data. But even though AI is becoming more common, the way people talk about it often seems hard to understand and full of technical terms. People talk a lot about terms like "artificial general intelligence" (AGI), even though they don't really exist yet. In this case, a recent comment by Mustafa Suleyman, the CEO of Microsoft AI, makes AI easier to understand in a more realistic way. He says that instead of thinking about definitions or what AI systems might be able to do in the future, we should look at what they can do right now and how those actions add value.This statement takes the conversation from theory to reality. It brings up a simple but important point: instead of arguing about what AI might become, it's better to know what it can do right now.The quote is really about being clear. Suleyman says that when people talk about AI, they often get sidetracked by complicated definitions and predictions. In doing so, they ignore what AI can already do today."Artificial Capable Intelligence" is not a technical term for him; it's a way of thinking that works. It looks at how well something works in the real world instead of how well it could work in theory. In short, he wants people to judge AI by what it does.This means looking at how well AI does its job, how accurate it is, and how much value it adds. This makes AI easier to understand and easier to measure. The focus changes from thinking about what might happen in the future to what is happening right now.There have always been many different and sometimes unclear definitions of artificial intelligence. Artificial General Intelligence is one of the most talked-about terms. It means a system that can do any intellectual task that a person can do. But no one has made such a system yet, and experts are still arguing about what it would actually look like.This is why talks about AI can get abstract. People may talk about things that aren't real yet, which can make it hard to understand how technology is right now. Suleyman's statement directly addresses this issue by saying that these definitions are not the best way to understand AI today.By focusing on capability, Suleyman's approach makes the discussion easier. He says that instead of asking if AI is smart enough, we should ask what it can do right now.This means knowing how AI works in real life. For instance, can it quickly look at a lot of data? Can it help you make decisions or write? Can it do the same thing over and over again?Answering these questions makes it easier to see how far AI has come already. This method also makes the technology easier for people who don't have a technical background to understand.People don't always notice it, but AI is already being used in a lot of different ways. It powers streaming platforms' recommendation systems, helps filter out spam emails, and supports customer service through chat-based tools.AI is being used in offices to do things like entering data, making schedules, and doing basic analysis. This lets workers focus on tasks that require them to be creative and think critically.These examples show that AI is useful because it can do certain tasks quickly and well. It may not be as smart as a person in every way, but its ability to do some things is already making a difference.The effect of AI on work is one of the most important things Suleyman says. AI systems are getting better at doing things, and this is changing how work is done in many fields.AI is often used to help people do their jobs instead of taking them away. It can take care of tasks that are boring or take a long time, so workers can focus on more important things. This means that people will have to do different jobs instead of losing their jobs completely.AI tools can answer simple questions in customer service, while human agents can handle more complicated ones. AI can quickly process large datasets in fields that rely on data, which helps professionals make decisions more quickly.One important point in the quote is that AI should be judged by how well it works. It's better to look at what AI can do than to worry about whether it has reached a certain level of intelligence.This includes things like how much time it saves, how accurate its results are, and how it makes things run more smoothly. These are clear signs that can be seen and measured.This is especially important for businesses. It lets them know if putting money into AI is a good idea and how it helps them be more productive and grow.Suleyman's mention of the "value of doing" shows how important it is to take action. AI isn't useful just because it's a concept; it has to do something useful for it to be useful.This means looking at how AI helps people solve problems, make things better, and get real benefits. A clear and measurable benefit of an AI system is that it cuts down on the time it takes to do a task.Focusing on these results makes the conversation about AI more useful and relevant to daily life.AI's abilities are already clear in many fields. AI is being used in healthcare to help with diagnosis and look at medical data. In education, it helps make learning experiences that are unique to each student.AI systems are used in finance to find fraud and better manage risks. AI tools help with writing, editing, and recommending content in the media and content fields.All of these apps show that AI is useful because it can do certain tasks well. These are actual, quantifiable skills that correspond with Suleyman's viewpoint.People are talking about AI in a different way these days. In the past, people often talked about long-term possibilities and what might happen in the future. There is now more of a focus on real-world effects and current uses.This change is shown in Suleyman's statement. The conversation becomes more concrete and easier to follow when it focuses on abilities. It also shows people how AI is already changing their lives.This change is important because it helps bring technical experts and regular people closer together.One of the best things about Suleyman's method is that it makes a complicated subject easier to understand. By concentrating on AI's capabilities, it eliminates the necessity to comprehend intricate definitions.This makes AI easier for more people to use. People don't need to know all the technical details to see how it affects them. They can just look at how it's being used and what good things it does.This is very important now that AI is becoming a bigger part of everyday life.Mustafa Suleyman's quote gives us a useful way to think about AI in a world that is changing quickly. He shifts the focus from definitions to capabilities, which brings out what really matters: how AI systems affect the world.This method tells people to focus on concrete results and real-world uses instead of getting lost in abstract ideas. It gives a better idea of how AI is changing industries, jobs, and the value it brings.This point of view is helpful as AI keeps changing. It reminds us that the real value of technology isn't what it's called, but what it can do.
Article Summary: Microsoft ai ceo mustafa suleyman says we should think of ai as "artificial capable intelligence" he says that instead of thinking about what ai systems might be able to do in the future, we should look at what they can do right now. This makes ai easier to understand and easier to measure.

Article Title: Trump Phone gets a facelift, sheds its ‘Made in USA’ claim
Article Text:
Trump Mobile has updated its gold T1 Phone with a new design and specifications, including a triple camera and AMOLED display. However, the 'Made in USA' claim has softened to 'shaped by American innovation.' Priced at $499 with a $100 deposit, the phone's launch timeline remains a mystery, with a 'Join the Waitlist' prompt replacing any release date.

Trump Phone 's " Made in USA " promise has quietly evaporated

T1 Phone pricing stays at $499, for now

Watch Trump Mobile Launch Ignites Meme Storm | Patriotic Power Move Or Just Another Cash Grab? Trump Mobile Launch Ignites Meme Storm | Patriotic Power Move Or Just Another Cash Grab?





Trump Mobile has overhauled its website and revealed a refreshed design for the T1 Phone, but the golden smartphone remains as elusive as ever with no launch timeline in sight.The redesigned handset is still gold—because of course it is—now featuring a triple vertical camera array, a cleaner American flag on the rear, and a "Trump Mobile" wordmark on the camera bump. Gone is the oversized "T1" branding that plastered the back of earlier renders. The phone looks largely identical to what Trump Mobile executives Eric Thomas and Don Hendrickson showed The Verge over a video call back in February, now rendered in higher resolution on the revamped site.Specs have been updated too. The T1 now lists a 6.78-inch AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate, a 50MP main camera alongside an 8MP ultrawide and 50MP 2x telephoto, a 50MP selfie camera, 512GB storage, a 5,000mAh battery with 30W charging, and an unspecified Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 series chip running Android 15.The "Made in USA" branding, which was the phone's original headline claim at launch in June 2025, has been progressively walked back with each website update. The T1 is now described as "shaped by American innovation" with "American teams helping guide design and quality"—a long way from the original pitch.Executives previously told The Verge that final assembly would happen in Miami, with most manufacturing done in a "favored nation" overseas.The phone is listed at a "promotional price" of $499, with $100 deposits still being accepted. That price will rise after the promotional window closes, though Trump Mobile hasn't said by how much—only that it will stay under $1,000. More notably, any mention of a release date has been stripped from the site entirely, replaced with a simple "Join the Waitlist" prompt.
Article Summary: Trump mobile has updated its website with a refreshed design and specifications for the t1 phone. However, the phone's launch timeline remains a mystery, with a 'join the waitlist' prompt replacing any release date. The 'made in usa' claim has softened to'shaped by american innovation' priced at $499 with a $100 deposit, the company hasn't said how much the phone will cost.

Article Title: Tim Cook's glasses might be a clue to what Apple's smart glasses will look like
Article Text: Apple's smart glasses are coming in 2027, and Tim Cook's own frames might be a preview. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports the company is testing at least four styles for its first pair, codenamed N50—one of which mirrors the slim rectangular glasses Cook wears. Built from premium acetate and designed entirely in-house, the display-free glasses will lean heavily on an upgraded Siri, expected to arrive with iOS 27.

Bloomberg's mark gurman reports the company is testing at least four styles for its first pair, codenamed n50. The display-free glasses will lean heavily on an upgraded siri, expected to arrive with ios 27.

Article Title: Celebrini Contract Projection: NHL agents suggest massive $20M AAV projection for Macklin Celebrini's next contract
Article Text:
Macklin Celebrini future extension is being viewed as a potential market-resetting deal. (Image Via Twitter)

NHL star Macklin Celebrini might land a ‘monster deal’ worth $20 million AAV in his next contract

Watch Sharks' Macklin Celebrini Fires Power-Play Goal Through Traffic Vs. Oilers Sharks' Macklin Celebrini Fires Power-Play Goal Through Traffic Vs. Oilers

NHL star Macklin Celebrini could be the next potential player for a record-setting payday, as speculations are doing the rounds that he might land a staggering $20 million average annual value (AAV) deal in the future. In The Athletic’s annual agent poll, Celebrini’s next contract is already being viewed as a potential market-resetter, despite the young forward still being on his entry-level deal with the San Jose Sharks.Reports suggest that the 19-year-old is eligible for an extension in 2026, and expectations are rapidly escalating around what that deal could look like. It is important to note that Macklin Celebrini is signed to a 3-year, $2,925,000 contract with a cap hit of $975,000 per season, playing for the San Jose Sharks. NHL star Macklin Celebrini is already generating a massive buzz around his long-term earning potential, with league insiders suggesting that the San Jose Sharks star could secure a “monster deal” worth up to $20 million annually on his next contract.According to reports based on NHL agent surveys, Celebrini’s future extension is being viewed as a potential market-resetting deal. It is to be noted that his contract expires at the end of the 2026-27 season, making Celebrini a Restricted Free Agent.Speaking about the possibility, the agent stated, “He’s going to get a monster deal, and they’re going to have to pay him. What Celebrini is doing in San Jose is incredible. It’s been a slow burn with the second contracts being so big. This is going to take them to another level, which will reshape the way teams get squeezed with their high-end guys.”Reports suggest that shorter deals may fall closer to the $10–12 million AAV range, while long-term commitments could climb significantly higher. However, if Celebrini continues his current trajectory, some agents believe a $20 million AAV deal is within reach.Celebrini deserves such an amount as he has emerged as a promising player in recent times with his great playmaking skills. He has recently finished fourth in this league, behind stars such as Connor McDavid and Nathan MacKinnon, recording 112 points, including 44 goals and 68 assists, in 80 games. That made him just the sixth teenager in league history to hit 100 points, joining names like Sidney Crosby and Wayne Gretzky.As for now, Celebrini is on his entry-level deal through 2026–27, giving the team short-term flexibility. However, the extension talks could begin in 2026, as expectations are already sky-high.
Article Summary: Macklin celebrini future extension is being viewed as a potential market-resetting deal. Reports suggest that shorter deals may fall closer to the $10–12 million aav range, while long-term commitments could climb significantly higher. The 19-year-old is on his entry-level deal through 2026–27, giving the team short-term flexibility.

Article Title: Clavicular Livestream Clip: Clavicular situation takes worrying turn after livestream clip spreads and silence raises more concern
Article Text:
Clavicular (Image Via Getty)

Journalist Taylor Lorenz shares update on Braden Clavicular condition after overdose and seizure







The situation around Kick streamer Braden “Clavicular” quickly became serious on April 14, 2026. Clips from his livestream started spreading across X, Reddit, and TikTok. In those videos, he looked unresponsive while sitting back in a club. People watching the stream noticed something was wrong almost immediately. The moment created panic online as viewers tried to understand what had happened.Soon after, TMZ reported that the 20-year-old streamer had been taken to a hospital after a suspected overdose. Around the same time, an alleged 911 call also surfaced on X. In that audio, a dispatcher could be heard saying, “Rescue 16. Trying to respond. 20-year-old male. Overdose. 900, South Miami Avenue.” This confirmed that emergency help had been called to the location.Later that day, journalist Taylor Lorenz shared an update about Braden Clavicular’s health. She reported that he was in stable condition but still not fully conscious. According to her update, doctors planned to keep him under observation for at least 72 hours.She wrote, “Update on Clavicular: He is in stable condition but still in and out of consciousness.The hospital will likely keep him for 72 hours. He overdosed and had a seizure. Will share more updates as I receive.”Before the incident, another clip showed Clavicular drinking something from a small bottle during the livestream. In that same video, he spoke to people around him but seemed clearly unwell. He said, “I am Clav, nice to meet you. Sorry, I am trying my best to focus, but I am not feeling okay right now. I am trying my best. I need water.”Moments later, the camera moved away, and things appeared to get worse. That clip is now being widely shared online, with many viewers questioning what exactly he consumed.Clavicular has not issued an official announcement regarding his health as of yet. His staff has not even confirmed any more information. The circumstance is still evolving, and updates are anticipated after medical professionals finish their observation period.This incident has once again raised concerns about safety during livestreams, especially in uncontrolled public environments. Many viewers online are now asking for more awareness and responsibility from creators while streaming live content.
Article Summary: Journalist taylor lorenz shares update on braden clavicular condition after overdose and seizure. She reported that he was in stable condition but still not fully conscious. Doctors planned to keep him under observation for at least 72 hours. The incident has once again raised concerns about safety during livestreams.

Article Title: Vivo T5 Pro with 9,020 mAh battery and 90W fast charging support launched in India: Price, specs and more
Article Text:
Vivo T5 Pro: Price and availability



Vivo T5 Pro specifications







Vivo T5 Pro is now official. Vivo has expanded its T-series of smartphones with the launch of Vivo T5 Pro in India. The smartphone is powered by a Qualcomm chipset and features a 1.5K AMOLED display . Vivo T5 Pro is backed by a massive 9,020 mAh battery and the IP68 & IP69 rating makes the smartphone resistant to dust and water.With the T5 Pro, we’re elevating the Series T by delivering a dependable, high-performance smartphone built for today’s always-on consumers. As usage evolves with 4K content, larger apps, and AI-driven features, power demands have grown significantly. Featuring our biggest-ever battery, the T5 Pro addresses this need by reducing charge anxiety and enabling users to stay connected, create, and play without interruption—reflecting our commitment to enhancing everyday experiences,” said Pankaj Gandhi, chief business officer – Online, vivo India.The vivo T5 Pro 5G will be available in Glacier Blue and Cosmic Black, and will be priced at Rs 29,999 (8GB + 128GB, inclusive of all taxes), INR 33,999 (8GB + 256GB, inclusive of all taxes), and Rs 39,999 (12GB + 256GB, inclusive of all taxes). The smartphone will go on sale starting April 21, 2026, across Flipkart, vivo India e-store, and all partner retail stores.Consumers can avail of the following offers when the phone goes on sale*:* Up to Rs 3000 Instant Bank Discount ( HDFC, SBI, Axis ) / UPI, OR* Up to Rs 3000 Exchange Bonus* Up to 6 months of no-cost EMIVivo T5 Pro comes with a 6.83-inch AMOLED display with 1260x2800 pixel resolution.The display offers 144hz refresh rate and up to 1900 nits peak tightness levels. The smartphone is powered by an octa-core Qualcomm Snapdragon 7s Gen chipset. The smartphone packs up to 12GB of RAM and it comes in two storage options — 128GB and 256GB.Vivo T5 Pro runs Android 16 operating system topped with company’s own layer of OriginOS 16. The smartphone is equipped with a 50MP Sony IMX882 main camera, paired with a 32MP front camera for sharp, detailed selfies. AI-powered features such as AI Erase13 and AI UHD13 further refine images by removing unwanted elements and enhancing clarity, delivering polished, professional-looking results with minimal effort.Vivo T5 Pro houses a massive 9,020 mAh battery which comes with 90W FlashCharge support. The company claims that the smartphone can charge up to 50% charge in just 37 minutes.
Article Summary: Vivo t5 pro is powered by a qualcomm chipset and features a 1.5k amoled display. The smartphone is backed by a massive 9,020 mah battery and the ip68 & ip69 rating makes the smartphone resistant to dust and water. It will be available in glacier blue and cosmic black, and will be priced at rs 29,999 (8gb + 128gb)

Article Title: Jesse Dwyer: Perplexity AI Chief Communications Officer Jesse Dwyer may have a 'message' for Google: Web search is 'primitive technology' and has not seen any innovation for 24 years
Article Text:
Perplexity believes the real breakthrough lies in technology itself



Perplexity argues that AI is more than just an upgrade



Ads, monetisation, and target users







The next battle in search may not be about ads or user behaviour, but about rewriting the technology itself. That’s the message from Perplexity AI, which is now positioning its platform as a fundamental rethink of how information is retrieved online. According to a report by Benzinga, Jesse Dwyer, Chief Communications Officer at Perplexity, said: “Search, as most people know it, is a primitive technology that didn’t experience any real innovation for 24 years.” He added that the arrival of AI-native tools made the disruption “obvious.”While most of the industry debate is centred around user behaviour and monetisation, but Perplexity believes that the real breakthrough lies in technology itself. The company frames that the web as the world’s largest hard drive, where the “write” function has long been solved, but the “read” function has lagged. AI, it says, finally makes that read function possible.Perplexity sees AI as more than just an upgrade to queries. The traditional systems take instructions, whereas the AI systems take objectives. Instead of returning links, they aim to deliver direct answers, reshaping what users expect from computing.“As the computer is now evolving, what users ask and do with it also evolves,” Dwyer explained.Unlike its rivals like Google , OpenAI and Meta, which are also exploring ad-driven models, Perplexity it is focusing on serving the “curious decision-makers whose choices can be “GDP-altering or history-making.” Dwyer noted: “It seems reasonable to assume we should have no problem making money with those people as our most passionate users.”Early hype branded Perplexity as a “Google killer,” but the company is distancing itself from that narrative. Instead, it highlights its edge in accurate AI and “massively multi-model orchestration,” which it claims go beyond simply being AI-native.
Article Summary: Perplexity ai is positioning its platform as a fundamental rethink of how information is retrieved online. The company frames the web as the world’s largest hard drive, where the “write” function has long been solved, but the ‘read’ function has lagged. The traditional systems take instructions, whereas the ai systems take objectives.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.
